info.title: Bodhveda Notifications API
info.description: API for sending notifications to recipients or broadcasting to all.

post.description: Start sending notifications through Bodhveda API.

post.summary: Add a contact to a recipient
post.description: Register a per-medium contact address for a recipient. Email needs a **primary** email contact to receive email. A duplicate `(medium, address)`, or a second primary for the same `(recipient, medium)`, returns `409`.

parameters.name: recipient_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the recipient.

x-codeSamples.label: cURL
x-codeSamples.source:
curl -X POST https://api.bodhveda.com/recipients/recipient_123/contacts \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer bv_xxxxxxxxx" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "medium": "email",
    "address": "alice@example.com",
    "is_primary": true
  }'

x-codeSamples.label: JavaScript
Go

409.description: A contact with this address, or a primary contact for this medium, already exists.

put.description:
Idempotently ensure an address is the recipient's **primary** contact for a medium — create it if absent, update the existing primary in place if the address differs (which resets `verified_at`), or no-op if it already matches. Returns the resulting primary contact (`200`) either way, so a "keep the primary email current" sync is a single call.

💡 Sync this **server-side** so the address never rides a browser request.

parameters.name: recipient_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the recipient.

x-codeSamples.source:
import (
    "context"

    bodhveda "github.com/MudgalLabs/bodhveda/sdk/go"
)

ctx := context.Background()
client := bodhveda.NewClient("bv_xxxxxxxxx", nil)

err := client.Recipients.Contacts.Delete(ctx, "recipient_123", 1)

get.description:
Every `(target, medium)` known for this recipient, each with the state a send would **actually** act on.

`state.enabled` is the **resolved** decision — the same answer the delivery path computes — not a raw stored row. Resolution walks, in order:

1. the recipient's rule for the exact target,
2. the recipient's `topic: any` rule for the channel/event,
3. the project's rule for the exact target,
4. the project's `topic: any` rule,
5. the medium's **default**.

**The default is per medium:** `in_app` delivers, every other medium does not. A `topic: none` target never takes an `any` rule.

The response covers every target in the project catalog **plus** any target this recipient has a rule of their own for, across the active mediums (`in_app` and `email`). So a recipient can have an entry for a `(target, medium)` you never cataloged — if they have an explicit rule for it, it is live, and this reports it.

Each `state` carries:

-   `enabled` — whether a send to this `(target, medium)` would deliver.
-   `inherited` — `true` when the recipient has no rule of their own for this exact `(target, medium)`; the value came from elsewhere in the cascade. `false` means they set it explicitly.
-   `cataloged` — whether a project-level rule exists for this exact `(target, medium)`. This is **context, not a gate**: an explicit recipient rule on an uncataloged pair still delivers, and `in_app` delivers by default with no catalog entry at all. Use it to decide what to render, never to predict delivery — `enabled` is the answer.

parameters.name: recipient_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the recipient.

patch.summary: Set recipient's subscription state for a target
patch.description: Set a recipient's subscription state for a specific target.

parameters.name: recipient_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the recipient.

medium.description: The medium this preference applies to. In-app and email are toggled independently for the same target. Defaults to `in_app` when omitted.

enabled.description: Whether the recipient is subscribed to this target for this medium.

x-codeSamples.label: cURL
JavaScript
Go
React

200.description: Updated subscription state for the target

get.summary: Check recipient's subscription state for a target
get.description:
Resolve a single `(target, medium)` for a recipient: whether a send would **actually** deliver.

`state.enabled` is the resolved decision, computed by the same cascade the delivery path uses — the recipient's exact rule → the recipient's `topic: any` rule → the project's exact rule → the project's `topic: any` rule → the medium's default (`in_app` delivers, every other medium does not). A `topic: none` target never takes an `any` rule.

The target does **not** need to be in your catalog; any `(channel, topic, event)` resolves. `state.cataloged` reports whether a project-level rule exists for this exact `(target, medium)` — context, not a gate.

parameters.name: recipient_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the recipient.

parameters.name: channel
parameters.description: Channel to check.

parameters.name: topic
parameters.description: Topic to check.

parameters.name: event
parameters.description: Event to check.

parameters.name: medium
parameters.description: Which medium to check the subscription state for. Defaults to `in_app`.

x-codeSamples.label: cURL
x-codeSamples.source:
curl -G https://api.bodhveda.com/recipients/recipient_123/preferences/check \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer bv_xxxxxxxxx" \
  --data-urlencode "channel=posts" \
  --data-urlencode "topic=post_id_123" \
  --data-urlencode "event=new_comment" \
  --data-urlencode "medium=email"

get.description: Retrieve a paginated list of notifications for a recipient, ordered by most recent first.

parameters.name: recipient_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the recipient.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: Returns results before the specified cursor. Use this to paginate backwards or fetch earlier items in the list.

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: Returns results after the specified cursor. Use this to paginate forwards or fetch newer items in the list.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of notifications to return.

200.description: List of notifications for the recipient.

patch.description: Updates the state of one or more notifications for the specified recipient.

parameters.name: recipient_id
parameters.description: Unique external identifier for the recipient.

ids.description: Array of notification IDs to update. If omitted, applies to all.

state.description:
The notification state to update. 
- You can set one or multiple state fields in the same request. 
- Fields left out of `state` will remain unchanged.

read.description: Whether the notification is marked as read.

opened.description: Whether the notification is marked as opened.

ids.description: Array of notification IDs to delete. If omitted, all will be deleted.

get.description: List every entry in the project's preference **catalog**. The project is taken from the API key. Requires a `Full access` key.

x-codeSamples.label: cURL
x-codeSamples.source:
curl https://api.bodhveda.com/preferences \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer bv_xxxxxxxxx"

x-codeSamples.label: JavaScript
Go
x-codeSamples.source:
import (
    "context"

    bodhveda "github.com/MudgalLabs/bodhveda/sdk/go"
)

ctx := context.Background()
client := bodhveda.NewClient("bv_xxxxxxxxx", nil)

preferences, _ := client.Preferences.List(ctx)

post.description: Create a single catalog entry. **Strict**: an entry for the same `(channel, topic, event, medium)` returns `409`. Use `PATCH /preferences/{preference_id}` to change an existing entry, or `PUT /preferences` to set a whole catalog.

x-codeSamples.label: cURL
x-codeSamples.source:
curl -X POST https://api.bodhveda.com/preferences \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer bv_xxxxxxxxx" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "channel": "digest",
    "topic": "none",
    "event": "sent",
    "medium": "email",
    "name": "Daily digest",
    "description": "Receive a daily summary of activity.",
    "default_enabled": true
  }'

x-codeSamples.label: JavaScript
Go
x-codeSamples.source:
import (
    "context"

    bodhveda "github.com/MudgalLabs/bodhveda/sdk/go"
)

ctx := context.Background()
client := bodhveda.NewClient("bv_xxxxxxxxx", nil)

pref, _ := client.Preferences.Create(ctx, &bodhveda.CreateProjectPreferenceRequest{
    Channel:        "digest",
    Topic:          "none",
    Event:          "sent",
    Medium:         bodhveda.MediumEmail,
    Name:           "Daily digest",
    Description:    "Receive a daily summary of activity.",
    DefaultEnabled: true,
})

409.description: An entry for this `(channel, topic, event, medium)` already exists.

put.description:
Declaratively merge a whole catalog in one call — the primitive for a one-off "set up my project's preferences" script. Each item is upserted by its natural key (`channel, topic, event, medium`): new entries are inserted, existing ones have their label and default updated. Entries **not** in the array are left untouched, unless `prune=true`.

Returns the full resulting catalog.

parameters.name: prune
parameters.description: When `true`, catalog entries absent from the array are **deleted**, making the array the entire desired catalog. Pruning un-catalogs a `(target, medium)`, which turns a non-`in_app` medium off for recipients relying on the default — so it is opt-in.

x-codeSamples.label: cURL
JavaScript
Go

BearerAuthWithAPIKeyWithFullAcessScope.description: Bearer authentication header of the form `Bearer bv_xxxxxxxxx`, where `bv_xxxxxxxxx` is your **API Key** with `Full access` scope.

BearerAuthWithAPIKeyWithEitherScope.description: Bearer authentication header of the form `Bearer bv_xxxxxxxxx`, where `bv_xxxxxxxxx` is your **API Key** with `Full access` or `Recipient acess` scope.

recipient_id.description:
The `id` of a recipient.
- If null → this is treated as a **broadcast** notification.
- If set → this is a **direct** notification to that recipient. 

 💡 If no recipient exists with the `recipient_id`, Bodhveda will create a new recipient with that `recipient_id`.

payload.description: Arbitrary JSON payload for the notification. This is the **in-app** content block — its presence makes the notification eligible for in-app delivery.

EmailContent.description:
Optional typed **email** content block. Its **presence** is your signal that email is eligible for this send (content-block-implies-intent). Absence ⇒ no email — there is **no** fallback that derives email from `payload`.

- **Direct sends only.** Including an `email` block on a broadcast returns `400` (email is never sent on broadcasts).
- Bodhveda does **no** templating in v1 — you render your own subject/HTML/text (e.g. with `@react-email`) and pass the result.
- Email still fires only if the `(target, email)` pair is in the project catalog, the recipient's email preference is enabled, and the recipient has a primary email contact.

subject.description: The email subject line. Required when an `email` block is present.

html.description: Rendered HTML body. At least one of `html` or `text` must be provided.

text.description: Plain-text body. Recommended for deliverability. If omitted, it is derived from `html`.

NotificationDelivery.description: A per-medium delivery outcome for a **direct** send (email in v1). A failed medium never rejects the send — the outcome is reported here instead.

status.description: Delivery status. At send time one of `pending` (queued to the provider), `muted` (catalog/preference disallows email), `no_contact` (recipient has no primary email contact), or `failed` (e.g. email provider not configured). Later transitions to `sent`/`delivered`/`bounced`/`complained` via provider webhooks.

address.description: The address the delivery was sent to (captured at send time), when known.

failure_reason.description: Set when the medium did not send. E.g. `not_cataloged`, `preference_disabled`, `provider_not_configured`.

Target.description: A **target** is the structure used to describe _who should get a notification_ and _how it should be categorized_.

topic.description:
Subcategory inside the channel (e.g., "post_id_123"). 
- `any` and `none` have special meaning. 
- `any` means **all topics under this channel/event**. 
- `none` means **only the base channel/event**. 
- `any` is **not** a valid `topic` when sending a notification, but both are valid for preferences. 

 A preference with the target `posts:any:new_comment` will match a notification with target `posts:post_id_123:new_comment` but a preference with the target `posts:none:new_comment` will **only** match a notification with target `posts:none:new_comment`.

deliveries.description: Per-medium delivery outcomes for a **direct** send (email in v1). In-app is intentionally absent — its outcome lives on the `notification` object.

recipient_id.description: Unique identifier for the recipient who received this notification.

payload.description: JSON payload sent with the notification.

broadcast_id.description:
Unique identifier for the broadcast that created this notification. 
 It is `null`, if this is a **direct** notification.

read.description:
Whether the notification has been read by the recipient. 

 💡 You can update this to `true` or `false` via [mark notifications as read](/api-reference/endpoint/mark-read).

opened.description:
Whether the notification has been opened by the recipient. 

 💡 You can update this to `true` or `false` via [mark notifications as opened](/api-reference/endpoint/mark-opened).

id.description:
Unique identifier from your system. 

 This cannot be changed after creation. So use a stable identifier that will not change over time.

RecipientContact.description: A per-medium contact address for a recipient.

medium.description: The delivery transport this address is for. Only `email` is exercised today; `sms`, `web_push`, and `mobile_push` are reserved for future transports.

address.description: The contact address (e.g. an email address). Email addresses are stored lowercased.

is_primary.description: Whether this is the recipient's primary contact for its medium. Email is sent to the primary email contact.

verified_at.description: When the contact was verified, or `null` if unverified.

medium.description: The delivery transport. Only `email` is exercised today.

address.description: The contact address. For email it must contain `@` and is stored lowercased.

is_primary.description: Mark this as the primary contact for its medium. Only one primary is allowed per `(recipient, medium)`.

UpdateRecipientContactPayload.description: Provide at least one field. Changing the address resets `verified_at`.

is_primary.description: Set or unset this contact as the primary for its medium.

medium.description: The delivery transport. Only `email` is exercised today.

address.description: The address to make primary. For email it must contain `@` and is stored lowercased.

ProjectPreference.description: A project-level preference — one entry in the project's **catalog**. The catalog declares which `(target, medium)` pairs the project may send and the default a recipient inherits until they set a preference of their own.

id.description: Unique identifier for the catalog entry.

medium.description: The medium this catalog entry gates.

default_enabled.description: The project-level default for this `(target, medium)`: whether a recipient who has set no preference of their own is delivered to.

label.description: Human-readable label for the catalog entry.

topic.description: Subcategory. `any` matches all topics under the channel/event; `none` is the base channel/event.

medium.description: The medium this catalog entry gates. Defaults to `in_app`.

default_enabled.description: The project-level default for recipients who have set no preference of their own.

UpdateProjectPreferencePayload.description: Updates a catalog entry's label and default. The natural key (`channel`/`topic`/`event`/`medium`) is immutable.
